question: How to add responsive images in HTML
description: Serve the right image size for every device using srcset, sizes, and the <picture> element — reducing bandwidth without sacrificing quality.

steps.title: Use srcset for resolution switching
steps.explanation: srcset lets the browser choose the best image based on the device pixel ratio. Use 1x, 2x, 3x descriptors so retina screens get a sharper image automatically.

steps.title: Use srcset with sizes for viewport-based switching
steps.explanation: Use width descriptors (w) and a sizes attribute to tell the browser the intended display width at each breakpoint. The browser picks the smallest image that still looks sharp.

steps.title: Use <picture> for art direction
steps.explanation: <picture> lets you swap entirely different images (not just sizes) at different breakpoints — useful when a wide crop works on desktop but a tight crop works better on mobile.

steps.title: Add lazy loading and async decoding
steps.explanation: loading="lazy" defers off-screen images until the user scrolls near them, cutting initial page load. decoding="async" lets the browser decode the image off the main thread.
